question_text: Let 𝐴 be a 3 × 3 real matrix and let 𝐼3 be the 3 × 3 identity matrix. Which one of the following statements is NOT true?

options.A: If the row-reduced echelon form of 𝐴 is 𝐼3, then zero is not an eigenvalue of 𝐴
options.B: If zero is not an eigenvalue of 𝐴, then the row-reduced echelon form of 𝐴 is 𝐼3
options.C: If 𝐴 has three distinct eigenvalues, then the row-reduced echelon form of 𝐴 is 𝐼3
options.D: If the system of equations 𝐴𝒙= 𝒃 has a solution for every 3 × 1 real column vector 𝒃, then the row-reduced echelon form of 𝐴 is 𝐼3
